steps.title: Mark the introductory clause
steps.content: The sentence begins with a dependent clause: "While many video game creators strive to make their graphics ever more lifelike." A comma should separate that introductory clause from the independent clause that follows.

steps.title: Avoid an extra conjunction
steps.content: A and D add "but," which is unnecessary because "while" already supplies the contrast. B leaves out the needed comma. The version with only a comma after "lifelike" correctly separates the clauses. The answer is C.
